evidence_quote: To ensure continuity of operations and the ability to perform 24 hours/day and 7 days/week, each key position should have at least one alternate.
catalog_notes: WVU Comprehensive Emergency Operations Plan (Jan 2024, v2.1) contains a dedicated Section 6.0 'Continuity of Operations' with subsections 6.1 Chain of Command (orders of succession listing President through Executive Officer), 6.2 Personnel Availability and Redundancy (alternates for each key position), and 6.3 Primary and Alternate Locations (President's Conference Room in Stewart Hall primary; University Police Conference Room secondary; NOROP facility at Morgantown Municipal Airport as backup if the Monongalia County EOC is unavailable). Confirmed by reading the rendered PDF.

evidence_quote: Continuity of Operations, sometimes aligned with the recovery phase, is the effort to carry on or promptly resume critical functions. The University continuity plans are developed and enacted at the department level and complement this plan.
catalog_notes: Fairmont State University Emergency Operations Plan (Aug 2022) contains a dedicated 'RECOVERY PHASE AND CONTINUITY OF OPERATIONS' section and a 'CONTINUITY OF OPERATIONS (COOP) COORDINATION' subsection: the EOC assesses incident impact, COOP plans may be implemented and coordinated centrally, and a line of command and succession is established for continuity at the EOC. The Executive Policy Group is responsible for maintaining continuity of university operations. Confirmed by reading the rendered PDF.

framework_alignment: FEMA Continuity Guidance Circular (Alaska non-federal entity guidance)
evidence_quote: The Business and Academic Continuity of Operations Planning Program is designed to document a plan for recovery and resumption of critical functions within your department after an emergency has occurred.
catalog_notes: University of Alaska System Office of Risk Services operates a 'Business and Academic Continuity of Operations Planning Program' and publishes a downloadable 'Business and Academic Continuity of Operations Planning Template' (.doc at alaska.edu/risksafety/download/Business-and-Academic-Continuity-of-Operations-Planning-Template-1.doc) for departments to develop continuity plans for recovery/resumption of critical functions. Risk Services is charged with designing and implementing emergency preparedness, disaster and business continuity programs system-wide (UAA/UAF/UAS). Page resolves on official alaska.edu host; quote taken from the official program page (web_fetch rate-limited, so link-only).

evidence_quote: The Academic Impact Unit will address needs, concerns and issues related to academic impacts resulting from the emergency in concert with the Business Continuity Branch under the Operations Section.
catalog_notes: University of Montana EOP (2023-24) embeds continuity through a dedicated 'Business Continuity Branch' under the Operations Section and an 'Academic Impact Unit' that addresses business continuity from an academic perspective (suspension of classes, academic recovery plans, faculty/student record-keeping). The President's Policy Group evaluates 'functions deemed critical to the continuity of UM business operations' and the Recovery section addresses continuity of operations. Distinct institution from Montana State University. Confirmed by reading the rendered PDF.

framework_alignment: NIMS / ICS; all-hazards (L363 Multi-Hazards EM for Higher Ed)
evidence_quote: Continual MSU operations are critical when impacted by a large-scale incident. There are key principles that must be planned and practiced avoiding any significant disruptions with MSU operations.
catalog_notes: Missouri State University EOP (2023 Basic Plan) contains a dedicated Section VII 'CONTINUITY OF OPERATIONS' (BP 12) with subsections A 'Cancellation of Classes and Campus Closure' (academic continuity) and B 'Preservation of Records' (vital records). Distinct institution from the University of Missouri System already cataloged. Confirmed by reading the rendered PDF.

institution_name: University of Arkansas at Little Rock
framework_alignment: Business risk management / operational risk management (tiered IT recovery)
evidence_quote: The Business Continuity Plan (BCP) for the University of Arkansas at Little Rock (UALR) integrates business risk management, operational risk management, and business continuity.
catalog_notes: UA Little Rock Information Technology Services publishes a Business Continuity Plan (Third Revision, 2010) using a tiered approach to keep the university managed during a disaster that renders the technical infrastructure inoperable for a period exceeding two days. Distinct institution from University of Arkansas at Monticello already cataloged. Page resolves on official ualr.edu host; quote taken from the official ITS BCP page (web_fetch rate-limited, so link-only).
